input.prompt:
Inspect the Expo app to find all screens/components that can delete equipment, edit/update equipment images, replace equipment images, or save edited equipment. Also check if they use the cleanup-enabled functions (deleteEquipmentWithCleanup, updateEquipmentWithImageCleanup) or the old functions (deleteEquipment, updateEquipment).

1. Read services/cloudinaryService.ts to confirm cleanup functions exist
2. Read services/firestoreService.ts to see both old and new functions
3. Search for all usages of deleteEquipment, deleteEquipmentWithCleanup, updateEquipment, updateEquipmentWithImageCleanup across the entire app
4. Read any screen files that reference equipment deletion or update (likely in app/ directory)
5. Report exactly which screens call which functions

responseMessageContents.text: Let me inspect the relevant files to understand the current state of the app and whether cleanup functions are being used in screens.

input.prompt:
Inspect the Expo React Native app to find all screens/components that delete or edit/update equipment. I need to know:

1. Which files contain equipment deletion logic (button handlers, actions, etc.)
2. Which files contain equipment editing/updating logic (especially image changes)
3. Whether they call `deleteEquipmentWithCleanup` or `updateEquipmentWithImageCleanup` (the cleanup-enabled functions) or the plain `deleteEquipment`/`updateEquipment` functions

Search for:
- Any imports or calls to `deleteEquipment`, `deleteEquipmentWithCleanup`, `updateEquipment`, `updateEquipmentWithImageCleanup` across all files
- Any screen that has a delete button or edit/save for equipment
- Check services/cloudinaryService.ts and services/firestoreService.ts for the function definitions

Read the key files found and report exactly what functions are being called in each screen.

output.result: Error: could not find task tool entry in parent context.

1|import React, { useState, useRef, useEffect } from 'react';
     2|import { View, Text, StyleSheet, ScrollView, Pressable, Dimensions, Alert } from 'react-native';
     3|import { SafeAreaView } from 'react-native-safe-area-context';
     4|import { Image } from 'expo-image';
     5|import { ArrowLeft, ArrowRight, MapPin, Star, Calendar, Shield, Share2, Heart } from 'lucide-react-native';
     6|import { useLocalSearchParams, useRouter } from 'expo-router';
     7|import Colors from '@/constants/colors';
     8|import { useLanguage } from '@/contexts/LanguageContext';
     9|import { useAuth } from '@/contexts/AuthContext';
    10|import { mockCategories, mockCities } from '@/mocks/categories';
    11|import { fetchEquipmentById, fetchUserById, createRequest } from '@/services/firestoreService';
    12|import { Equipment, User } from '@/types';
    13|import { getImageUrl } from '@/utils/imageHelpers';
    14|
    15|const { width: SCREEN_WIDTH } = Dimensions.get('window');
    16|
    17|export default function EquipmentDetailScreen() {
    18|  const { id } = useLocalSearchParams<{ id: string }>();
    19|  const { isRTL, t, localizedText } = useLanguage();
    20|  const { user: currentUser } = useAuth();
    21|  const router = useRouter();
    22|  const [currentImage, setCurrentImage] = useState<number>(0);
    23|  const [liked, setLiked] = useState<boolean>(false);
    24|  const [equipment, setEquipment] = useState<Equipment | null>(null);
    25|  const [owner, setOwner] = useState<User | null>(null);
    26|  const [_loading, setLoading] = useState<boolean>(true);
    27|  const scrollRef = useRef<ScrollView>(null);
    28|
    29|  useEffect(() => {
    30|    let mounted = true;
    31|    const load = async () => {
    32|      try {
    33|        if (!id) return;
    34|        const eq = await fetchEquipmentById(id);
    35|        if (mounted && eq) {
    36|          setEquipment(eq);
    37|          const ownerUser = await fetchUserById(eq.ownerUid);
    38|          if (mounted) setOwner(ownerUser);
    39|        }
    40|      } catch (e) {
    41|        console.log('[EquipmentDetail] Error:', e);
    42|      } finally {
    43|        if (mounted) setLoading(false);
    44|      }
    45|    };
    46|    void load();
    47|    return () => { mounted = false; };
    48|  }, [id]);
    49|  const categoryObj = equipment ? mockCategories.find(c => c.id === equipment.category) : null;
    50|  const cityObj = equipment ? mockCities.find(c => c.id === equipment.city) : null;
    51|
    52|  const BackIcon = isRTL ? ArrowRight : ArrowLeft;
    53|
    54|  if (!equipment) {
    55|    return (
    56|      <View style={styles.container}>
    57|        <SafeAreaView edges={['top']} style={styles.safeArea}>
    58|          <Text style={styles.errorText}>{t('error_occurred')}</Text>
    59|        </SafeAreaView>
    60|      </View>
    61|    );
    62|  }
    63|
    64|  const title = localizedText(equipment.titleAr, equipment.titleEn);
    65|  const description = localizedText(equipment.descriptionAr, equipment.descriptionEn);
    66|  const cityName = cityObj ? localizedText(cityObj.nameAr, cityObj.nameEn) : equipment.city;
    67|  const categoryName = categoryObj ? localizedText(categoryObj.nameAr, categoryObj.nameEn) : equipment.category;
    68|  const ownerName = owner ? localizedText(owner.nameAr, owner.nameEn) : '';
    69|
    70|  const handleRequestRental = () => {
    71|    if (!currentUser || !equipment) return;
    72|    if (currentUser.uid === equipment.ownerUid) {
    73|      Alert.alert(t('error_occurred'), 'Cannot request your own equipment');
    74|      return;
    75|    }
    76|    Alert.alert(t('request_rental'), '', [
    77|      { text: t('cancel'), style: 'cancel' },
    78|      {
    79|        text: t('confirm'),
    80|        onPress: async () => {
    81|          try {
    82|            const days = 5;
    83|            const amount = equipment.pricePerDay * days;
    84|            const platformFee = Math.round(amount * 0.1);
    85|            await createRequest({
    86|              equipmentId: equipment.id,
    87|              customerUid: currentUser.uid,
    88|              providerUid: equipment.ownerUid,
    89|              status: 'pending',
    90|              startDate: new Date().toISOString(),
    91|              endDate: new Date(Date.now() + days * 86400000).toISOString(),
    92|              notes: '',
    93|              amount,
    94|              platformFee,
    95|              providerAmount: amount - platformFee,
    96|              paymentStatus: 'unpaid',
    97|              paymentId: '',
    98|              paidAt: null,
    99|              currency: 'SAR',
   100|              allowChat: false,
   101|            });
   102|            Alert.alert(t('success'), '', [
   103|              { text: t('confirm'), onPress: () => router.back() },
   104|            ]);
   105|          } catch (e) {
   106|            console.log('[EquipmentDetail] Request error:', e);
   107|            Alert.alert(t('error_occurred'), t('try_again'));
   108|          }
   109|        },
   110|      },
   111|    ]);
   112|  };
   113|
   114|  return (
   115|    <View style={styles.container}>
   116|      <ScrollView showsVerticalScrollIndicator={false} bounces={false}>
   117|        <View style={styles.imageCarousel}>
   118|          <ScrollView
   119|            ref={scrollRef}
   120|            horizontal
   121|            pagingEnabled
   122|            showsHorizontalScrollIndicator={false}

   124|              const index = Math.round(e.nativeEvent.contentOffset.x / SCREEN_WIDTH);
   125|              setCurrentImage(index);

   127|          >
   128|            {equipment.images.map((img, i) => (

   131|          </ScrollView>
   132|
   133|          <SafeAreaView edges={['top']} style={styles.imageOverlay}>

   137|              </Pressable>
   138|              <View style={[styles.imageActionsRight, { flexDirection: isRTL ? 'row-reverse' : 'row' }]}>
   139|                <Pressable style={styles.actionButton} onPress={() => setLiked(!liked)}>
   140|                  <Heart size={20} color={liked ? Colors.error : Colors.white} fill={liked ? Colors.error : 'transparent'} />
   141|                </Pressable>
   142|                <Pressable style={styles.actionButton}>
   143|                  <Share2 size={20} color={Colors.white} />
   144|                </Pressable>
   145|              </View>
   146|            </View>
   147|          </SafeAreaView>
   148|
   149|          <View style={styles.dotsContainer}>
   150|            {equipment.images.map((_, i) => (
   151|              <View key={i} style={[styles.dot, currentImage === i && styles.dotActive]} />
   152|            ))}
   153|          </View>
   154|
   155|          {equipment.availability ? (
   156|            <View style={styles.availableBadge}>
   157|              <Text style={styles.availableText}>{t('available')}</Text>
   158|            </View>
   159|          ) : (
   160|            <View style={styles.unavailableBadge}>
   161|              <Text style={styles.unavailableText}>{t('unavailable')}</Text>
   162|            </View>
   163|          )}
   164|        </View>
   165|
   166|        <View style={styles.content}>
   167|          <View style={[styles.titleRow, { flexDirection: isRTL ? 'row-reverse' : 'row' }]}>
   168|            <View style={{ flex: 1 }}>
   169|              <Text style={[styles.title, { textAlign: isRTL ? 'right' : 'left' }]}>{title}</Text>
   170|              <Text style={[styles.categoryText, { textAlign: isRTL ? 'right' : 'left' }]}>{categoryName}</Text>
   171|            </View>
   172|            <View style={styles.priceTag}>
   173|              <Text style={styles.priceValue}>{equipment.pricePerDay.toLocaleString()}</Text>
   174|              <Text style={styles.priceUnit}>{t('per_day')}</Text>
   175|            </View>
   176|          </View>
   177|
   178|          <View style={[styles.locationRow, { flexDirection: isRTL ? 'row-reverse' : 'row' }]}>
   179|            <MapPin size={16} color={Colors.gold} />
   180|            <Text style={styles.locationText}>{cityName} - {equipment.district}</Text>
   181|          </View>
   182|
   183|          <View style={styles.divider} />
   184|
   185|          <Text style={[styles.sectionTitle, { textAlign: isRTL ? 'right' : 'left' }]}>{t('description')}</Text>
   186|          <Text style={[styles.descriptionText, { textAlign: isRTL ? 'right' : 'left' }]}>{description}</Text>
   187|
   188|          <View style={styles.divider} />
   189|
   190|          {owner && (
   191|            <>
   192|              <Text style={[styles.sectionTitle, { textAlign: isRTL ? 'right' : 'left' }]}>{t('owner')}</Text>
   193|              <Pressable style={[styles.ownerCard, { flexDirection: isRTL ? 'row-reverse' : 'row' }]}>
   194|                <Image source={{ uri: owner.avatar }} style={styles.ownerAvatar} contentFit="cover" />
   195|                <View style={[styles.ownerInfo, { alignItems: isRTL ? 'flex-end' : 'flex-start' }]}>

   197|                    <Text style={styles.ownerName}>{ownerName}</Text>

   202|                    <Text style={styles.ownerRatingText}>{owner.rating} ({owner.totalRatings} {t('rating_count')})</Text>
   203|                  </View>

   205|                </View>

responseMessageContents.text: Now let me check if there's an edit equipment screen and the add/update flow:

results.line: export async function updateEquipment(id: string, updates: Partial<Equipment>): Promise<void> {

responseMessageContents.text:
Here's the analysis:

## Inspection Results

### Files Inspected
1. **`expo/app/my-equipment.tsx`** — Equipment list with delete & edit buttons
2. **`expo/app/equipment/[id].tsx`** — Equipment detail screen (view only, no edit/delete)
3. **`expo/app/(tabs)/add/index.tsx`** — Equipment creation (add tab)
4. **`expo/app/create-listing.tsx`** — Redirect to `/(tabs)/add`
5. **`expo/services/firestoreService.ts`** — All Firestore service functions

### Findings

#### 1. Equipment Deletion
- **`my-equipment.tsx` (line 54)** — Already calls `deleteEquipmentWithCleanup(item.id)` ✅
- This is the **only** place in the app where equipment is deleted
- Cloudinary cleanup is **already active** in the real user flow

#### 2. Equipment Update/Edit
- The "Edit" button in `my-equipment.tsx` navigates to `/equipment/[id]`, but that screen is **view-only** (detail page) — it has no edit form or update logic
- **No screen in the app currently calls `updateEquipment()` or `updateEquipmentWithImageCleanup()`**
- The `updateEquipmentWithImageCleanup()` function exists in `firestoreService.ts` but is **not imported or used anywhere**

#### 3. Equipment Creation
- `create-listing.tsx` redirects to `/(tabs)/add` which is the creation form
- Creation only adds new images — no old images to clean up, so no cleanup needed there ✅

### Summary

| Flow | Cleanup Active? | Notes |
|------|----------------|-------|
| Delete equipment | ✅ Yes | Uses `deleteEquipmentWithCleanup` |
| Edit/update equipment images | ⚠️ N/A | No edit screen exists yet — the edit button opens the detail/view page |
| Create equipment | ✅ N/A | No cleanup needed for new listings |

### Conclusion
- **No code changes needed.** Cloudinary cleanup is already active in the only real deletion flow.
- `updateEquipmentWithImageCleanup()` is ready and waiting in `firestoreService.ts` but has no consumer yet because an equipment edit form doesn't exist.
- **No path bypasses Cloudinary cleanup** — the only delete path uses the cleanup-enabled function.
- When an edit equipment screen is built in the future, it should import and use `updateEquipmentWithImageCleanup()` to handle image replacement cleanup.
